title: Image 2 of New-Haven daily herald (New Haven, Conn.), October 25, 1844
description: THE DAILY HERALD PUBLISHED BY WOODWARD CARRINGTON 115 CHAPELSTss PER ANNUM a Friday October 25 1444 FOR PRESIDENT HENRY CLAY of Kentucky 7 FOR VICE PRESIDENT Theodore Frelinghuysen of New Jersey For Presidential Electors CLARK BISSELL of Norwalk CHARLES W ROCKWELL of Norwich SAMUEL A FOOT of Cheshire Nathaniel o Kellogg of Vernon JOSEPH S GLADDING of Plainfield Truman smith of Litchfield WHIG PRINCIPLES A sound National currency regulated by the will and authority of the Nation An adequate Revenue with fair protection to American Industry Just restraints on the Executive power em bracing a further restriction on the exercise of the veto A faithful administration of the Public domain with an equitable distribution of the proceeds of Sales of it among all the States An honest and economical administration of the general Government leaving public officers per ect freedom of thought and of the right ofsuf rige but with suitable restraints against improp r interference in elections An amendment of the Constitution limiting the incumbent of the Presidential office to a single term S 3 We regret that a communication re rding the Democratic Rally last eve erfiing and the meeting of the Whigs at the Saloon the evening
